_comment: Source config for run.py. 'rss' feeds are fetched headlessly (public feeds only; on any block the run records a Source Blocked row, never bypasses). 'ai_browser' sources are captured by the conductor via Chrome plugin (Codex) or Claude-in-Chrome/Computer Use (Claude). Edit search URLs to taste.
_ai_browser_comment:
Capture EVERY source in this list each run with the local logged-in browser. Craigslist is listed here (not only under rss) because its RSS 403s headlessly but loads fine in a real browser. Extraction playbook for Craigslist list pages: read .cl-search-result cards -> a[href], .posting-title .label (title), .priceinfo (rent), .result-posted-date (posted), .result-location (city); to pull the full result set past the tool's output truncation, write the JSON array into document.body as 
@@BEGIN@@...@@END@@
then read it with get_page_text. Facebook Marketplace defaults to the account's saved city (Liam's is Seattle) -> open the location dialog, type 'Santa Clara, California', pick the City option, Apply; the URL then carries latitude=37.3505&longitude=-121.9427&radius= (use ~24). Capture VISIBLE facts only; never bypass CAPTCHA/login/rate limits; never message posters or submit anything.

ai_browser.name: Craigslist South Bay rooms
ai_browser.note: Rooms/shares near office. Filter to Santa Clara/Sunnyvale/Mountain View/North San Jose. Flexible+cheap = top priority.

ai_browser.name: Craigslist South Bay sublets
ai_browser.note: Sublets/temporary near office = the most-wanted lease shape. Drop RV/boat/storage and ultra-short stays.

ai_browser.name: Craigslist South Bay apartments
ai_browser.note: Apartments near office in the solo budget band. Filter to Santa Clara/Sunnyvale/Mountain View/North San Jose.

ai_browser.name: Craigslist SF sublets
ai_browser.note: SF lifestyle options. Capture the neighborhood precisely (Caltrain bike-distance drives SF scoring).

ai_browser.name: Craigslist Peninsula sublets
ai_browser.note: Caltrain-corridor mid-points (San Mateo, Redwood City, Palo Alto).

ai_browser.name: Facebook Marketplace (South Bay rentals)
ai_browser.note: Logged-in visible browser. If listings show another metro, set location to 'Santa Clara, California' (see playbook). Cards: a[href*='/marketplace/item/'] -> price/beds-or-title/city lines. Subleases/rooms are top priority.

ai_browser.name: Facebook housing groups (Bay Area sublets/rooms)
ai_browser.note: Groups like 'Bay Area Housing, Rooms, Sublets & Roommates'. Capture visible post facts only; do not message posters. Park if the group feed is noisy/unreachable.

ai_browser.name: Zillow Rentals (Santa Clara/Sunnyvale, max $4500)
ai_browser.note: Apply price filter in the UI; capture rent, beds, lease, address, url. Heavy anti-bot: if it challenges, record Source Blocked and move on.

ai_browser.name: Kopa furnished sublets (near SCU/Stanford)
ai_browser.note: Furnished short-term sublets; strong for early-career movers.

ai_browser.name: Zumper (Santa Clara, max $4000)
ai_browser.note: Apartments/condos with lighter anti-bot than Zillow; capture rent, beds, address, url.

_apis_comment: Free/keyed JSON endpoints fetched by capture_api.py. No-key entries (e.g. Reddit) run headlessly in the cloud; keyed entries skip until their key_env is set. {city} in url is substituted from each 'cities' value. We never add proxy/CAPTCHA/stealth code — heavy anti-bot portals (Zillow/Apartments/FB) go through the LOCAL browser instead.

apis.name: Reddit
apis.note: Public Reddit JSON; best-effort (may rate-limit cloud IPs -> recorded as Source Blocked). No login/key.

apis.name: RentCast
apis.note: Optional, OFF by default. Legit rentals/condos API with a free tier (~50 calls/mo). Set RENTCAST_API_KEY + enabled:true to use; listings have no public URL so they land in Needs Verification.
